text.title: Tiny Bets Quarterly Cadence Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Tiny Bets Quarterly Cadence application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Each quarter MUST commit to ≤4 bets total. Five or more = hedging; the cadence fails because no bet gets enough attention to be falsified.
rule.rationale: Tiny-seed / indie-portfolio practice — anchors Tiny Bets Quarterly Cadence rule `max-four-bets`.

rule.statement: Each bet MUST have a written kill-criterion (metric + threshold + observation window) BEFORE committing. Bets without a kill-criterion run forever.
rule.rationale: Pre-mortem / falsifiability discipline — anchors Tiny Bets Quarterly Cadence rule `kill-criterion-pre-commit`.

rule.statement: At mid-quarter (week 6), every bet MUST be graded against its kill-criterion. Bets failing the criterion MUST be killed or paused; sunk-cost continuation is rejected.
rule.rationale: Mid-quarter review practice — anchors Tiny Bets Quarterly Cadence rule `mid-quarter-cull`.

rule.statement: Every bet MUST name a single accountable owner (the operator themselves, or a named collaborator). 'Team' / 'we' / 'us' fails.
rule.rationale: Single-throat-to-choke practice — anchors Tiny Bets Quarterly Cadence rule `named-owner-required`.

rule.statement: Each quarter MUST close with a rollup naming: bets shipped, bets killed, revenue delta, lessons. Without the rollup, the next quarter starts blind.
rule.rationale: Operating-rhythm research — anchors Tiny Bets Quarterly Cadence rule `end-of-quarter-rollup`.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
